job_spec.original_text:
Role: Senior AI Engineer - Agentic Talent Scouting

We are building an AI-powered talent scouting and engagement agent for recruiters. The agent should parse job descriptions, discover matching candidates, score them with explainable match logic, simulate conversational outreach, and return a ranked shortlist that a recruiter can act on immediately.

Responsibilities:
- Build production-quality Python services and clean recruiter-facing workflows.
- Design AI agents with tool calling, RAG, candidate matching, ranking, and evaluation loops.
- Create transparent scoring for Match Score and Interest Score.
- Work with candidate data responsibly, including privacy and human-in-the-loop review.

Requirements:
- 3+ years of experience building AI or ML products.
- Strong Python and FastAPI.
- Experience with LLMs, RAG, embeddings, vector databases, and prompt engineering.
- Bonus: recruiting, talent sourcing, ATS integrations, LangGraph, observability, and privacy.

Location: Remote or Bengaluru hybrid.
job_spec.title: AI Engineer
responsibilities: Build production-quality Python services and clean recruiter-facing workflows.
Design AI agents with tool calling, RAG, candidate matching, ranking, and evaluation loops.
recruiter_questions: Can the candidate show a working ai engineer project with real users or realistic data?
What evidence proves the candidate can ship an end-to-end prototype quickly?
Can they explain trade-offs around human-in-the-loop, tool calling, evaluations?
Do they understand domain-specific risks in hrtech?
Are they comfortable with the role's remote operating model?

sources: technical blogs, demo videos, and talks (simulated)
filters: evidence required: project, achievement, or public artifact
resume evidence parsed into candidate profiles

candidate.name: Ishaan Kapoor
candidate.headline: Builds agentic AI products with strong Python, FastAPI, LangGraph, RAG, embeddings, vector databases, and recruiter-facing UX.
projects: Built a resume-to-shortlist agent that parsed JDs, normalized skills, ranked candidates, and generated recruiter notes.
Shipped a LangGraph support agent with tool calling, trace logs, and human approval before actions.
achievements: Reduced screening time by 35% in a pilot workflow.
Created evaluation tests for extraction quality, ranking quality, and hallucination risk.
Preferences: open to remote or Bengaluru hybrid, likes ownership and agentic AI.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.message: Hi Ishaan, I noticed your work on built a resume-to-shortlist agent that parsed jds, normalized skills, ranked candidates, and generated recruiter notes. We are hiring for AI Engineer and the role needs human-in-the-loop, tool calling, evaluations. Would you be open to a quick exploratory chat?

transcript.speaker: candidate
transcript.message: Yes, this is relevant. I am especially interested in agentic ai, and the AI Engineer scope sounds close to work I have already shipped.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.message: Helpful. Before I shortlist you, I want to confirm three things: hands-on production evidence, availability, and what would make the role worth your time.

transcript.speaker: candidate
transcript.message: My notice period is 30 days and compensation expectation is around 36 LPA. The strongest pull is motivated by agentic ai. Main concern: validate logistics and expectations.

interview_questions: Walk me through a shipped project where you used prompt engineering to solve a real user problem.
What would your first version of this talent-scouting agent do in week one, and what would you intentionally leave out?
How would you evaluate whether the agent's shortlist is actually useful to recruiters?
Show the trade-offs you would make around human-in-the-loop for speed versus reliability.
Where have you built something closest to scoring models, even if the stack name was different?
How would you prevent the agent from overusing sensitive candidate information?

risk_signals.label: Skill validation needed
risk_signals.rationale: Missing direct evidence for scoring models, privacy.
risk_signals.mitigation: Ask for a shipped example involving scoring models before final shortlist.

next_steps: Send recruiter screen invite within 24 hours.
Ask for proof around human-in-the-loop in a shipped project.
ranked_shortlist.counterfactual: Would move up materially if they can show hands-on evidence for scoring models.

candidate.headline: Agentic AI engineer building production recruiting copilots
projects: Built a hiring-copilot that parsed JDs, searched an internal talent graph, and generated recruiter-ready shortlists.

recruiter_brief.hiring_thesis: For AI Engineer, prioritize candidates with shipped agent/RAG evidence, recruiting-domain pull, and enough availability to enter process quickly.
recruiter_brief.shortlist_strategy: Interview Ishaan Kapoor first, compare against Aanya Rao, and use risk mitigations to avoid wasting panel time.
recommended_sequence: Start with Ishaan Kapoor because combined score and confidence are strongest.
Use candidate-specific interview questions instead of a generic screen.
Validate the highest-severity risk before scheduling deep technical panels.
Keep the next two candidates warm as backups with tailored outreach hooks.
compliance_audit: Scores use skills, evidence, domain context, logistics, and stated preferences.
Protected attributes such as gender, age, religion, caste, and ethnicity are not model inputs.
Location is used only as a logistics constraint and should be reviewed by a human recruiter.
Outreach is simulated in this prototype; production use should require consent-aware messaging.
demo_talking_points: This is an auditable agent workflow, not a single black-box prompt.
The system separates role match from candidate interest, then combines them transparently.
Every top candidate includes evidence paths, risk mitigations, transcript, and next-best action.
The discovery layer is swappable for GitHub, ATS, CRM, or a consented talent graph.

summary.recommended_action: Start with Ishaan Kapoor; they have the strongest combined evidence and interest signal.
risk_flags: Top candidates still have at least one skill gap to validate.

audit_log: Received job description and initialized scouting run.
Parsed JD into title=AI Engineer, seniority=senior, skills=10 must-have/9 nice-to-have.
Parsed 3 user-provided resume profile(s) and included the simulated market.
Discovered 12 candidate profiles from resume and market sources.
Computed match, interest, and combined ranking scores with explainable breakdowns.
Generated evidence paths, confidence scores, risk mitigations, and interview questions.
Returned top 5 candidates with next actions for the recruiter.
